headline: Southwest Charger Limit Highlights Tension Between Safety Rules And Customer Appeal
summary: Southwest Airlines (NYSE:LUV) has introduced a new rule limiting each traveler to one portable charger. The airline describes the move as a safety measure, even though UN guidance allows passengers to carry two. This sets Southwest apart from other major U.S. carriers, which generally follow the looser global standard. Southwest Airlines, a major U.S. low cost carrier focused on domestic routes, is adjusting its policies at a time when passengers rely heavily on personal devices for work...

headline: Is Southwest (LUV) Using Wine-Country Perks to Quietly Redefine Its Leisure-Focused Network Strategy?
summary: Southwest Airlines recently began service at Santa Rosa’s Charles M. Schulz Sonoma County Airport, linking the wine country region with nonstop flights to San Diego, Las Vegas, Denver, and Burbank, and launched its Sip and Ship program letting customers check one case of wine at no extra cost from select West Coast airports. This expansion into a wine tourism hub, paired with a unique baggage perk tailored to leisure travelers, highlights how Southwest is experimenting with differentiated...

headline: Ceasefire Ignites Wall Street's Best Day in a Year: Oil Craters, Cyclicals Surge, and the Iran Clock Is Still Ticking
summary: Wednesday handed investors something they hadn't seen since early 2025: a genuine risk-on session where almost everyone won, unless you happened to own energy stocks. A last-minute US-Iran ceasefire brokered by Pakistan sent oil off a cliff and lifted every sector that had been crushed by rising energy costs.
